test_cases.description: Verify that api/openapi.yaml exists, uses OpenAPI 3.1.0 format with YAML syntax, and is parseable by libopenapi without structural errors.
preconditions: Repository root is accessible
api/openapi.yaml file exists at the repository root
libopenapi Go module is available via go.mod
test_cases.input: Read api/openapi.yaml bytes and pass to libopenapi NewDocument
test_cases.expected: NewDocument returns no error; BuildV3Model returns a non-nil model and an empty errors slice; the parsed openapi field equals '3.1.0'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: bytes = readFile('api/openapi.yaml'); doc, err = NewDocument(bytes); assert err == nil; model, errs = doc.BuildV3Model(); assert len(errs) == 0; assert model.Model.Info != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that api/openapi.yaml defines a top-level servers block with exactly one entry: url '/api/v1' and the correct description.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect the top-level servers array
test_cases.expected: servers array has exactly one element; element.url == '/api/v1'; element.description == 'Default mount point (configurable per deployment)'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); servers = model.Servers; assert len(servers) == 1; assert servers[0].URL == '/api/v1'; assert servers[0].Description == 'Default mount point (configurable per deployment)'

test_cases.description: Verify that /healthz, /readyz, and /version each have a path-level servers override with url '/' to resolve as absolute paths.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect path items for /healthz, /readyz, /version
test_cases.expected: Each of the three paths has a path-level servers array with exactly one entry where url == '/'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for path in ['/healthz', '/readyz', '/version']: pathItem = model.Paths[path]; assert pathItem.Servers != nil; assert len(pathItem.Servers) == 1; assert pathItem.Servers[0].URL == '/'

test_cases.description: Verify that all $ref references in api/openapi.yaml resolve without errors, indicating correct use of components/schemas.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: Validator exits with code 0 and reports no unresolved $ref errors
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: exitCode = runCommand('go run github.com/pb33f/libopenapi-validator/cmd/openapi-validator@latest api/openapi.yaml'); assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that a single bearerAuth HTTP bearer security scheme is defined under components/securitySchemes with a description covering all three credential types.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: components.securitySchemes.bearerAuth exists; type == 'http'; scheme == 'bearer'; description contains references to 'admin', 'API Key', and 'PAT' credential types
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); scheme = model.Components.SecuritySchemes['bearerAuth']; assert scheme != nil; assert scheme.Type == 'http'; assert scheme.Scheme == 'bearer'; assert 'admin' in scheme.Description; assert 'PAT' in scheme.Description

test_cases.description: Verify that all authenticated and admin endpoints include bearerAuth in their security array.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and enumerate all operations except health probes and GET /auth/providers
test_cases.expected: Every authenticated operation has a security array containing at least one entry referencing bearerAuth
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); publicPaths = ['/healthz', '/readyz', '/version', '/auth/providers']; for each operation not in publicPaths: assert 'bearerAuth' in operation.Security

test_cases.description: Verify that health probe endpoints and GET /auth/providers are public with no bearerAuth security requirement and no 401 response defined.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect security and responses for GET /healthz, GET /readyz, GET /version, GET /auth/providers
test_cases.expected: Each of these four operations has an empty or absent security array and no 401 response entry
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for op in [GET /healthz, GET /readyz, GET /version, GET /auth/providers]: assert op.Security == nil or len(op.Security) == 0; assert '401' not in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that the PAT permissions array item schema uses pattern '^[a-z_]+:[a-z_]+$' and does not use an enum property.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect the permissions item schema in PatMetadata and Pat schemas
test_cases.expected: permissions items schema has pattern == '^[a-z_]+:[a-z_]+$'; no enum property present on the item schema
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for schemaName in ['PatMetadata', 'Pat']: schema = model.Components.Schemas[schemaName]; permItems = schema.Properties['permissions'].Items; assert permItems.Pattern == '^[a-z_]+:[a-z_]+$'; assert permItems.Enum == nil or len(permItems.Enum) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that the PAT permissions item schema description documents all six built-in permission strings.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect the description of the permissions item schema
test_cases.expected: Description contains 'users:read', 'orgs:read', 'keys:read', 'keys:manage', 'tokens:read', 'tokens:manage'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); permItems = model.Components.Schemas['PatMetadata'].Properties['permissions'].Items; desc = permItems.Description; for perm in ['users:read', 'orgs:read', 'keys:read', 'keys:manage', 'tokens:read', 'tokens:manage']: assert perm in desc

test_cases.description: Verify that the expires field on POST /auth/callback and POST /user/tokens uses enum [0,30,60,90], default 90, with 0 documented as permanent.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect the expires property in POST /auth/callback and POST /user/tokens request body schemas
test_cases.expected: expires schema has enum == [0, 30, 60, 90]; default == 90; description states 0 means permanent/no expiry with expires_at null
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for path in ['POST /auth/callback', 'POST /user/tokens']: expiresSchema = getRequestBodyProperty(path, 'expires'); assert expiresSchema.Enum == [0, 30, 60, 90]; assert expiresSchema.Default == 90; assert '0' in expiresSchema.Description and 'null' in expiresSchema.Description

test_cases.description: Verify that expires_at fields in all relevant schemas are declared nullable with format date-time.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect expires_at in ApiKeyMetadata, ApiKey, PatMetadata, Pat, and AuthCallbackResponse schemas
test_cases.expected: All expires_at fields allow null values (nullable: true or type: [string, null]) and have format: date-time
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for schemaName in ['ApiKeyMetadata', 'ApiKey', 'PatMetadata', 'Pat']: expiresAt = model.Components.Schemas[schemaName].Properties['expires_at']; assert isNullable(expiresAt); assert expiresAt.Format == 'date-time'

test_cases.description: Verify that the PATCH /user request body schema contains only full_name and does not include username or email.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect the request body schema for PATCH /user
test_cases.expected: Request body schema has exactly one property 'full_name'; properties 'username' and 'email' are absent
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); schema = getRequestBodySchema('PATCH', '/user'); assert set(schema.Properties.keys()) == {'full_name'}; assert 'username' not in schema.Properties; assert 'email' not in schema.Properties

test_cases.description: Verify that the PATCH /users/{id} request body schema contains only full_name and does not include username or email.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect the request body schema for PATCH /users/{id}
test_cases.expected: Request body schema has exactly one property 'full_name'; properties 'username' and 'email' are absent
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); schema = getRequestBodySchema('PATCH', '/users/{id}'); assert set(schema.Properties.keys()) == {'full_name'}; assert 'username' not in schema.Properties; assert 'email' not in schema.Properties

test_cases.description: Verify that both PATCH /user and PATCH /users/{id} operation descriptions state that username and email are immutable via PATCH.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect operation description for PATCH /user and PATCH /users/{id}
test_cases.expected: Both operation descriptions contain text indicating username and email are immutable and cannot be changed via PATCH
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for op in [getOperation('PATCH', '/user'), getOperation('PATCH', '/users/{id}')]: assert 'immutable' in op.Description.lower() or 'username' in op.Description

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /healthz is defined with path-level servers override, no auth, 200 response with status ok, Cache-Control no-cache, and X-Request-ID header.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: Path has servers [{url: '/'}]; operation has no security; 200 response body schema has status='ok'; Cache-Control header value is 'no-cache'; X-Request-ID header is defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/healthz'); assert pathItem('/healthz').Servers[0].URL == '/'; assert op.Security == nil; assert getResponseSchema(op, '200').Properties['status'].Enum == ['ok']; assert getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'Cache-Control').Schema.Enum contains 'no-cache'; assert getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'X-Request-ID') != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /readyz defines both 200 (status ok) and 503 (status unavailable) responses with appropriate headers.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: Path has servers [{url: '/'}]; no security; 200 response has {status: ok}; 503 response has {status: unavailable}; both responses include Cache-Control: no-cache and X-Request-ID
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/readyz'); assert '200' in op.Responses; assert '503' in op.Responses; assert getResponseSchema(op, '503').Properties['status'].Enum contains 'unavailable'; assert getResponseHeader(op, '503', 'X-Request-ID') != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /version defines a 200 response with version, build_time, commit, and mount_point fields plus appropriate headers.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect GET /version 200 response schema
test_cases.expected: 200 response body schema has all four string fields: version, build_time, commit, mount_point; Cache-Control: no-cache; X-Request-ID defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/version'); schema = getResponseSchema(op, '200'); for field in ['version', 'build_time', 'commit', 'mount_point']: assert field in schema.Properties; assert schema.Properties[field].Type == 'string'

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /auth/providers is public, returns array of OAuthProvider objects, has Cache-Control public max-age=300, and no 401 response.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: No security requirement; 200 response is array of objects with name and authorize_url; Cache-Control header value contains 'public, max-age=300'; no 401 in responses
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/auth/providers'); assert op.Security == nil or len(op.Security) == 0; assert '401' not in op.Responses; assert getResponseSchema(op, '200').Type == 'array'; assert getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'Cache-Control') references 'public'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /auth/callback has correct required fields, expires enum, 200 AuthCallbackResponse, 400 error, Cache-Control no-store, and X-Request-ID.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect POST /auth/callback request body and responses
test_cases.expected: Required fields: provider, code, redirect_uri; expires is optional integer enum [0,30,60,90] default 90; 200 response matches AuthCallbackResponse with user and api_key; 400 defined; Cache-Control: no-store; X-Request-ID defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('POST', '/auth/callback'); reqSchema = getRequestBodySchema(op); assert 'provider' in reqSchema.Required; assert 'code' in reqSchema.Required; assert 'redirect_uri' in reqSchema.Required; assert reqSchema.Properties['expires'].Enum == [0,30,60,90]; assert '200' in op.Responses; assert '400' in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /user has bearerAuth, returns User object with ETag, supports 304 conditional response with X-Request-ID and ETag but no body.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 200 returns User object; ETag response header on 200; 304 defined with X-Request-ID and ETag headers and no content; 401 and 403 responses present; Cache-Control: no-store
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/user'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '200' in op.Responses; assert '304' in op.Responses; assert '401' in op.Responses; assert '403' in op.Responses; assert getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'ETag') != nil; assert getResponseHeader(op, '304', 'X-Request-ID') != nil; assert getResponseContent(op, '304') == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that PATCH /user has bearerAuth, accepts only full_name in request body, and defines all required response codes.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; request body has only full_name; responses 200, 400, 401, 403, 415 all defined; Cache-Control: no-store; X-Request-ID header present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('PATCH', '/user'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); schema = getRequestBodySchema(op); assert set(schema.Properties.keys()) == {'full_name'}; for code in ['200', '400', '401', '403', '415']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /user/keys has bearerAuth, returns array of ApiKeyMetadata objects with nullable fields, and documents 401 and 403.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 200 response is array of ApiKeyMetadata with key_id, created_at, nullable expires_at, nullable revoked_at; 401 and 403 defined; Cache-Control: no-store
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/user/keys'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '200' in op.Responses; assert '401' in op.Responses; assert '403' in op.Responses; responseSchema = getResponseSchema(op, '200'); assert responseSchema.Type == 'array'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /user/keys/{key_id}/refresh has bearerAuth, returns ApiKey object with key field, and documents 401, 403, 404.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 200 response schema has key, key_id, and nullable expires_at; 401, 403, 404 defined; Cache-Control: no-store
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('POST', '/user/keys/{key_id}/refresh'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); schema = getResponseSchema(op, '200'); assert 'key' in schema.Properties; assert 'key_id' in schema.Properties; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /user/keys/{key_id} has bearerAuth, returns 204 with no body, and documents 401, 403, 404.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 204 response has no content body; 401, 403, 404 defined; Cache-Control: no-store
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('DELETE', '/user/keys/{key_id}'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '204' in op.Responses; assert getResponseContent(op, '204') == nil; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /user/tokens has bearerAuth, returns array of PatMetadata objects with all required fields, and documents 401 and 403.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 200 response is array of PatMetadata with token_id, name, permissions, created_at, nullable expires_at, nullable revoked_at; 401 and 403 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/user/tokens'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '200' in op.Responses; responseSchema = getResponseSchema(op, '200'); assert responseSchema.Type == 'array'; for code in ['401', '403']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /user/tokens has bearerAuth, correct request body, 201 response with Pat object including token field, and all error codes.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; request body requires name and permissions; expires is optional enum [0,30,60,90] default 90; 201 response schema has token, token_id, name, permissions, nullable expires_at; 400, 401, 403, 415 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('POST', '/user/tokens'); reqSchema = getRequestBodySchema(op); assert 'name' in reqSchema.Required; assert 'permissions' in reqSchema.Required; assert '201' in op.Responses; schema = getResponseSchema(op, '201'); assert 'token' in schema.Properties; for code in ['400', '401', '403', '415']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /user/tokens/{token_id} has ETag, supports 304 conditional response with X-Request-ID and ETag but no body, and documents all error codes.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 200 response has ETag header; 304 response has X-Request-ID and ETag headers and no content; 401, 403, 404 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/user/tokens/{token_id}'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'ETag') != nil; assert '304' in op.Responses; assert getResponseContent(op, '304') == nil; assert getResponseHeader(op, '304', 'ETag') != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /user/tokens/{token_id} has bearerAuth, returns 204 with no body, and documents 401, 403, 404.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 204 response with no content; 401, 403, 404 defined; Cache-Control: no-store; X-Request-ID header defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('DELETE', '/user/tokens/{token_id}'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '204' in op.Responses; assert getResponseContent(op, '204') == nil; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /user/orgs has bearerAuth with orgs:read, returns array of Organization objects, and documents 401 and 403.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; description mentions orgs:read PAT permission; 200 response is array of Organization objects; 401 and 403 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/user/orgs'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert 'orgs:read' in op.Description or 'orgs:read' in op.Summary; assert '200' in op.Responses; for code in ['401', '403']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /users has admin bearerAuth, required request body fields, 201 created user, and 409 conflict for duplicate username/provider.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; request body requires username, email, provider, provider_id; 201 response returns User object; 409 response defined; 400, 401, 403, 415 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('POST', '/users'); reqSchema = getRequestBodySchema(op); for field in ['username', 'email', 'provider', 'provider_id']: assert field in reqSchema.Required; assert '201' in op.Responses; assert '409' in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /users has admin bearerAuth, include_blocked boolean query param defaulting to false, 200 User array, and documented filtering behavior.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; include_blocked query parameter exists with type boolean and default false; 200 response is array of User objects; 401 and 403 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/users'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); param = getQueryParam(op, 'include_blocked'); assert param != nil; assert param.Schema.Type == 'boolean'; assert param.Schema.Default == false; assert '200' in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /users/{id} has admin bearerAuth, ETag header, 304 conditional response with X-Request-ID and ETag and no body, and all response codes.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; ETag on 200 response; 304 with X-Request-ID and ETag headers and no content; 401, 403, 404 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/users/{id}'); assert getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'ETag') != nil; assert '304' in op.Responses; assert getResponseContent(op, '304') == nil; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that PATCH /users/{id} has admin bearerAuth, only full_name in request body, and all required response codes.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; request body schema has only full_name property; 200, 401, 403, 404, 415 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('PATCH', '/users/{id}'); schema = getRequestBodySchema(op); assert set(schema.Properties.keys()) == {'full_name'}; for code in ['200', '401', '403', '404', '415']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /users/{id}/promote has admin bearerAuth, returns 200 User object, and documents 401, 403, 404.

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('POST', '/users/{id}/promote'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '200' in op.Responses; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('POST', '/users/{id}/unblock'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '200' in op.Responses; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /users/{id}/keys has admin bearerAuth, returns array of ApiKeyMetadata objects, and documents 401, 403, 404.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/users/{id}/keys'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '200' in op.Responses; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /users/{id}/keys/{key_id} has admin bearerAuth, 204 no body, and documents 401, 403, 404.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 204 response with no content; 401, 403, 404 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('DELETE', '/users/{id}/keys/{key_id}'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '204' in op.Responses; assert getResponseContent(op, '204') == nil; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /users/{id}/tokens has admin bearerAuth, returns array of PatMetadata objects, and documents 401, 403, 404.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: bearerAuth in security; 200 response is array of PatMetadata objects; 401, 403, 404 defined
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/users/{id}/tokens'); assert hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'); assert '200' in op.Responses; for code in ['401', '403', '404']: assert code in op.Responses

test_cases.description: Verify that the User schema in components/schemas has all required fields with correct types, status and role enums, and nullable full_name.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.expected: User schema has id (uuid string), username, email, full_name (nullable), status (enum active/blocked), role (enum admin/user), provider, provider_id, created_at and updated_at (date-time strings)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); schema = model.Components.Schemas['User']; for field in ['id', 'username', 'email', 'full_name', 'status', 'role', 'provider', 'provider_id', 'created_at', 'updated_at']: assert field in schema.Properties; assert isNullable(schema.Properties['full_name']); assert schema.Properties['status'].Enum == ['active', 'blocked']; assert schema.Properties['role'].Enum == ['admin', 'user']

test_cases.description: Verify that the PatMetadata schema has all required fields with permissions using pattern constraint.

test_cases.description: Verify that the Pat schema (creation) has token field with plaintext secret, token_id, name, permissions, and nullable expires_at.

test_cases.description: Verify that the AuthCallbackResponse schema has user ($ref to User) and api_key object with key, key_id, and nullable expires_at.

test_cases.description: Verify that every applicable endpoint documents all relevant error responses (400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 413, 415, 500) using the Error schema.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and enumerate all operations with expected error codes
test_cases.expected: Each operation that can produce a given error code has that status code defined in its responses using the Error schema $ref
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for each operation in model: for each expectedErrorCode in getExpectedErrorCodes(operation): assert expectedErrorCode in operation.Responses; assert operation.Responses[expectedErrorCode] references '#/components/schemas/Error'

test_cases.description: Verify that all authenticated endpoints explicitly document both 401 and 403 responses using the Error schema.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and find all operations with bearerAuth in security
test_cases.expected: Every operation with bearerAuth security has both 401 and 403 responses referencing the Error schema
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for each op in model where hasSecurity(op, 'bearerAuth'): assert '401' in op.Responses; assert '403' in op.Responses; assert op.Responses['401'] references Error schema; assert op.Responses['403'] references Error schema

test_cases.description: Verify that 413 Payload Too Large is documented on all endpoints that accept a request body, with the Error schema.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and find all operations with a requestBody defined
test_cases.expected: Every operation with a requestBody has a 413 response entry referencing the Error schema
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for each op in model where op.RequestBody != nil: assert '413' in op.Responses; assert op.Responses['413'] references Error schema

test_cases.description: Verify that all mutable resource endpoints document Cache-Control: no-store response header.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect Cache-Control header on all /user, /users/*, /orgs/*, key, and token operations
test_cases.expected: All mutable resource operations include a Cache-Control response header with value 'no-store' on their 200/201/204 responses
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); mutablePaths = all paths except /healthz, /readyz, /version, /auth/providers; for each op in mutablePaths: for each successResponse in op.Responses where code in [200, 201, 204]: cacheControl = getResponseHeader(successResponse, 'Cache-Control'); assert cacheControl != nil; assert 'no-store' in cacheControl.Schema.Enum or cacheControl.Schema.Example == 'no-store'

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /healthz, GET /readyz, and GET /version document Cache-Control: no-cache.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect Cache-Control header on health probe operations
test_cases.expected: Each of GET /healthz, GET /readyz, GET /version has a Cache-Control response header with value 'no-cache'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for path in ['GET /healthz', 'GET /readyz', 'GET /version']: op = getOperation(path); cacheControl = getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'Cache-Control'); assert cacheControl != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /auth/providers documents Cache-Control: public, max-age=300.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect Cache-Control header on GET /auth/providers
test_cases.expected: GET /auth/providers has Cache-Control response header with value containing 'public' and 'max-age=300'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); op = getOperation('GET', '/auth/providers'); cacheControl = getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'Cache-Control'); assert cacheControl != nil; assert 'public' in cacheControlHeaderDescription and 'max-age=300' in cacheControlHeaderDescription

test_cases.description: Verify that X-Request-ID UUID header is defined on every response across all operations and all status codes.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and enumerate every response across all operations
test_cases.expected: Every response (200, 201, 204, 304, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 413, 415, 500, 503) on every operation defines an X-Request-ID header
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for each op in model: for each statusCode, response in op.Responses: xRequestId = response.Headers['X-Request-ID']; assert xRequestId != nil; assert xRequestId.Schema.Type == 'string'

test_cases.description: Verify that ETag is documented as an opaque string header (no format/pattern) on exactly the four single-resource GET endpoints.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect ETag header on GET /user, GET /users/{id}, GET /user/tokens/{token_id}, GET /orgs/{id}
test_cases.expected: Each of the four endpoints has an ETag response header with schema type string and no format or pattern constraint
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for path in ['GET /user', 'GET /users/{id}', 'GET /user/tokens/{token_id}', 'GET /orgs/{id}']: op = getOperation(path); etag = getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'ETag'); assert etag != nil; assert etag.Schema.Type == 'string'; assert etag.Schema.Format == '' or etag.Schema.Format == nil; assert etag.Schema.Pattern == '' or etag.Schema.Pattern == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Content-Type constraints are documented: request bodies require application/json, response bodies use application/json; charset=utf-8, and 204/304 have no body.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect requestBody media types, response media types, and 204/304 response content
test_cases.expected: All requestBody definitions use 'application/json' media type; all response bodies use 'application/json; charset=utf-8' or 'application/json'; 204 and 304 responses have no content
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for each op: if op.RequestBody: assert 'application/json' in op.RequestBody.Content; for code, response in op.Responses: if code in ['204', '304']: assert response.Content == nil or len(response.Content) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that 304 responses on the four conditional GET endpoints have X-Request-ID and ETag headers, no body, and no Cache-Control header.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect 304 responses on GET /user, GET /users/{id}, GET /user/tokens/{token_id}, GET /orgs/{id}
test_cases.expected: Each 304 response has X-Request-ID and ETag headers defined; no response content; no Cache-Control header defined on the 304 response itself
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); for path in ['GET /user', 'GET /users/{id}', 'GET /user/tokens/{token_id}', 'GET /orgs/{id}']: op = getOperation(path); r304 = op.Responses['304']; assert r304.Headers['X-Request-ID'] != nil; assert r304.Headers['ETag'] != nil; assert r304.Content == nil; assert 'Cache-Control' not in r304.Headers

test_cases.description: Verify that all timestamp fields across all schemas use format: date-time and descriptions specify RFC 3339 UTC with Z suffix.
preconditions: api/openapi.yaml exists and is valid OpenAPI 3.1.0
test_cases.input: Parse api/openapi.yaml and inspect all timestamp fields in User, ApiKeyMetadata, ApiKey, PatMetadata, Pat, Organization schemas
test_cases.expected: All fields named created_at, updated_at, expires_at, revoked_at use type: string and format: date-time; descriptions or spec info mentions UTC and Z suffix
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: model = parseOpenAPI('api/openapi.yaml'); timestampFields = ['created_at', 'updated_at', 'expires_at', 'revoked_at']; for schemaName in ['User', 'ApiKeyMetadata', 'ApiKey', 'PatMetadata', 'Pat', 'Organization']: schema = model.Components.Schemas[schemaName]; for field in timestampFields: if field in schema.Properties: assert schema.Properties[field].Format == 'date-time'

test_cases.description: Verify that api/api_test.go exists and contains a Go test that reads and validates api/openapi.yaml using libopenapi, asserting no structural errors.
preconditions: Repository root is accessible
api/api_test.go exists at the api/ directory
Go toolchain is installed
libopenapi is listed in go.mod
test_cases.input: Read api/api_test.go source and run 'go test ./api/...'
test_cases.expected: File exists; contains at least one test function; imports libopenapi; calls ReadFile on api/openapi.yaml; calls NewDocument; calls BuildV3Model; asserts no errors; go test exits 0 when spec is valid
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: source = readFile('api/api_test.go'); assert 'libopenapi' in source; assert 'openapi.yaml' in source; assert 'BuildV3Model' in source or 'NewDocument' in source; exitCode = runCommand('go test ./api/...'); assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that api/api_test.go does not assert on individual operations, response examples, or ETag derivation — scope is structural parsing only.
preconditions: api/api_test.go exists
test_cases.input: Read api/api_test.go source and inspect test logic
test_cases.expected: Test source does not contain assertions on specific operation semantics, ETag values, or response example validation; it only checks the error/errs return from the libopenapi parsing calls
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: source = readFile('api/api_test.go'); assert 'ETag derivation' not in source; assert no assertions on specific operationIDs or paths beyond structural parsing errors; logically: test body calls parse function and checks len(errs) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that the Go validation test is discovered and executed as part of 'go test ./...' from the repository root.
preconditions: Repository root is accessible
Go toolchain installed
api/api_test.go exists with a valid test function
api/openapi.yaml is valid
test_cases.expected: Test output includes the api package test results; exit code is 0 when spec is valid; test name appears in output
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: output, exitCode = runCommand('go test ./...'); assert exitCode == 0; assert 'api' in output or 'PASS' in output

test_cases.description: Verify that 'make check' runs the openapi-validator against api/openapi.yaml and fails with non-zero exit code when the spec has errors.
preconditions: Repository root is accessible
Makefile defines a check target
Go toolchain installed
api/openapi.yaml exists
test_cases.input: Run 'make check' with a valid api/openapi.yaml; then run with a deliberately broken spec
test_cases.expected: With valid spec: make check exits 0. With invalid spec: make check exits non-zero and openapi-validator error output is present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: exitCode = runCommand('make check'); assert exitCode == 0; corruptSpec(); exitCode2 = runCommand('make check'); assert exitCode2 != 0

property_tests.description: For any ETag-returning endpoint in the spec, verify the ETag schema is typed as an opaque string with no format, pattern, or derivation algorithm specified.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all operations in the parsed api/openapi.yaml that define an ETag response header on their 200 response
property_tests.invariant_check: for each such operation: etag = getResponseHeader(op, '200', 'ETag'); assert etag.Schema.Type == 'string'; assert etag.Schema.Format == nil or etag.Schema.Format == ''; assert etag.Schema.Pattern == nil or etag.Schema.Pattern == ''; assert 'hash' not in (etag.Description or '').lower(); assert 'algorithm' not in (etag.Description or '').lower()

property_tests.description: For any operation with bearerAuth security, verify it defines both a 401 and a 403 response each referencing the Error schema.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all operations in the parsed api/openapi.yaml that have bearerAuth in their security array
property_tests.invariant_check: for each such operation: assert '401' in op.Responses; assert '403' in op.Responses; assert op.Responses['401'] uses Error schema ref; assert op.Responses['403'] uses Error schema ref

property_tests.description: For every response across all operations and all status codes, verify that X-Request-ID header is defined as a string.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all (operation, statusCode, response) triples in the parsed api/openapi.yaml
property_tests.invariant_check: for each (op, statusCode, response): xReqId = response.Headers['X-Request-ID']; assert xReqId != nil; assert xReqId.Schema.Type == 'string'

property_tests.description: For any schema modeling PAT permission strings, verify it uses pattern constraint and not an enum.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all schemas in components/schemas that contain a 'permissions' array property
property_tests.invariant_check: for each such schema: items = schema.Properties['permissions'].Items; assert items.Pattern == '^[a-z_]+:[a-z_]+$'; assert items.Enum == nil or len(items.Enum) == 0

property_tests.description: For any PATCH operation in the spec, verify the request body schema contains exactly one property (full_name) and does not include username or email.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all operations with HTTP method PATCH in the parsed api/openapi.yaml
property_tests.invariant_check: for each PATCH operation: schema = getRequestBodySchema(op); assert set(schema.Properties.keys()) == {'full_name'}; assert 'username' not in schema.Properties; assert 'email' not in schema.Properties

property_tests.description: For any schema containing an expires_at field, verify it is declared nullable with format date-time.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all schemas in components/schemas and all inline schemas in operation request/response bodies that contain an 'expires_at' property
property_tests.invariant_check: for each such schema with expires_at: field = schema.Properties['expires_at']; assert isNullable(field); assert field.Format == 'date-time'

property_tests.description: For any health probe path (/healthz, /readyz, /version), verify the path item defines a servers block with url '/' overriding the top-level servers.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate path items for /healthz, /readyz, /version in the parsed api/openapi.yaml
property_tests.invariant_check: for each health probe path item: assert pathItem.Servers != nil; assert len(pathItem.Servers) >= 1; assert pathItem.Servers[0].URL == '/'

property_tests.description: For every error response in the spec, verify the Error schema code field is documented as an integer equal to the HTTP status code with no sub-codes.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Inspect the Error schema in components/schemas and all error response entries referencing it
property_tests.invariant_check: errorSchema = model.Components.Schemas['Error']; codeField = errorSchema.Properties['error'].Properties['code']; assert codeField.Type == 'integer'; assert 'sub' not in (codeField.Description or '').lower(); assert 'HTTP' in (errorSchema.Description + codeField.Description) or 'status' in (errorSchema.Description + codeField.Description)

property_tests.description: For any 304 Not Modified response on conditional GET endpoints, verify it has no body, has X-Request-ID and ETag headers, and does not define Cache-Control.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate 304 responses on GET /user, GET /users/{id}, GET /user/tokens/{token_id}, GET /orgs/{id} in the parsed api/openapi.yaml
property_tests.invariant_check: for each 304 response: assert response.Content == nil or len(response.Content) == 0; assert response.Headers['X-Request-ID'] != nil; assert response.Headers['ETag'] != nil; assert 'Cache-Control' not in response.Headers

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a $ref pointing to a non-existent components/schemas entry causes the OpenAPI validator to report an unresolved reference error and make check to fail.
preconditions: A copy of api/openapi.yaml is modified to contain a $ref pointing to '#/components/schemas/NonExistentSchema'
make check target is configured to run openapi-validator
edge_case_tests.input: api/openapi.yaml with a $ref to '#/components/schemas/NonExistentSchema' substituted for a valid ref
edge_case_tests.expected: openapi-validator reports an unresolved reference error; make check exits with non-zero exit code; go test ./... also fails
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: modifiedSpec = injectBadRef('api/openapi.yaml', '$ref: "#/components/schemas/NonExistentSchema"'); writeFile('api/openapi.yaml', modifiedSpec); exitCode = runCommand('make check'); assert exitCode != 0; exitCode2 = runCommand('go test ./...'); assert exitCode2 != 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when libopenapi returns structural errors from a malformed api/openapi.yaml, the test calls t.Fatalf with error details and go test exits non-zero.
preconditions: api/api_test.go exists with structural validation logic
A malformed version of api/openapi.yaml is placed on disk (e.g. invalid YAML syntax or missing openapi version field)
edge_case_tests.input: api/openapi.yaml with invalid YAML syntax (e.g. unmatched colon or missing required openapi field)
edge_case_tests.expected: go test ./api/... exits with non-zero code; test output contains the libopenapi parser error details from t.Fatalf
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: writeMalformedYAML('api/openapi.yaml'); output, exitCode = runCommand('go test ./api/...'); assert exitCode != 0; assert 'FAIL' in output; assert len(output) > 0 // error details from t.Fatalf

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when api/openapi.yaml cannot be read from disk, the test calls t.Fatalf with the file read error and exits non-zero without panicking.
preconditions: api/api_test.go exists
api/openapi.yaml is renamed or removed to simulate a missing file
edge_case_tests.input: api/openapi.yaml does not exist on disk when go test runs
edge_case_tests.expected: go test ./api/... exits with non-zero exit code; output contains 'FAIL' and a message about file read failure; process does not panic or call os.Exit directly
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: removeFile('api/openapi.yaml'); output, exitCode = runCommand('go test ./api/...'); assert exitCode != 0; assert 'FAIL' in output; assert 'panic' not in output.lower() // t.Fatalf used not panic

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: developer edits api/openapi.yaml, runs 'go test ./...', and the api/api_test.go validation test executes and passes.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'go test ./...' from the repository root with a valid api/openapi.yaml in place
expected_effects: Go test runner discovers and runs the test in api/api_test.go
Test reads api/openapi.yaml from disk successfully
libopenapi parses the file and returns no structural errors
Test asserts no errors and passes
go test exits with code 0
Output shows 'ok' or 'PASS' for the api package

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test verifying POST /auth/callback is fully specified with correct request schema, expires enum, and AuthCallbackResponse with nullable expires_at.
smoke_tests.trigger: Parse api/openapi.yaml and validate the POST /auth/callback operation definition
expected_effects: POST /auth/callback operation is present under /auth/callback path
Request body requires provider, code, redirect_uri strings
expires field is optional integer with enum [0, 30, 60, 90] and default 90
200 response references AuthCallbackResponse schema containing user and api_key with nullable expires_at
400 response is defined with Error schema
X-Request-ID header is defined on all responses
Cache-Control: no-store is defined on the 200 response

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test verifying conditional GET with ETag and 304 Not Modified is fully specified on GET /user.
smoke_tests.trigger: Parse api/openapi.yaml and validate GET /user operation for ETag and 304 conditional response support
expected_effects: GET /user 200 response defines ETag header as opaque string
GET /user 304 response is defined with X-Request-ID and ETag headers
GET /user 304 response has no content body
GET /user 304 response does not define Cache-Control header
GET /user operation description or parameter documentation references If-None-Match

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test verifying admin user lifecycle endpoints (POST /users, promote, demote) are all fully specified with correct response codes.
smoke_tests.trigger: Parse api/openapi.yaml and validate POST /users, POST /users/{id}/promote, POST /users/{id}/demote operations
expected_effects: POST /users requires username, email, provider, provider_id; returns 201 User; documents 409 for duplicates
POST /users/{id}/promote returns 200 User; documents 401, 403, 404
POST /users/{id}/demote returns 200 User; documents 409 for last-admin constraint; documents 401, 403, 404
All three operations require bearerAuth security
All three operations define Cache-Control: no-store and X-Request-ID on responses

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test verifying PAT creation (POST /user/tokens) and scoped use (GET /user/orgs) are fully specified.
smoke_tests.trigger: Parse api/openapi.yaml and validate POST /user/tokens and GET /user/orgs operations
expected_effects: POST /user/tokens requires name and permissions; permissions items use pattern '^[a-z_]+:[a-z_]+$'
POST /user/tokens returns 201 Pat schema with token (plaintext), token_id, name, permissions, nullable expires_at
POST /user/tokens expires field is optional integer enum [0,30,60,90] default 90
GET /user/orgs requires bearerAuth with orgs:read PAT permission documented
GET /user/orgs returns 200 array of Organization objects
GET /user/orgs documents 403 for insufficient permissions

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test verifying GET /orgs with include_blocked query parameter filtering behavior is fully specified.
smoke_tests.trigger: Parse api/openapi.yaml and validate GET /orgs operation with include_blocked parameter
expected_effects: GET /orgs defines include_blocked as an optional boolean query parameter with default false
GET /orgs description documents that include_blocked=false returns only status=active organizations
GET /orgs description documents that include_blocked=true includes status=blocked organizations
GET /orgs returns 200 array of Organization objects
GET /orgs requires admin bearerAuth; documents 401 and 403
GET /orgs defines Cache-Control: no-store and X-Request-ID on responses
